channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: The never-ending supply chain attacks worm into SAP npm packages, other dev tools
item.description: Recent supply chain attacks have targeted SAP and Intercom npm packages, spreading credential-stealing malware. This highlights ongoing vulnerabilities in developer tools.

item.title: Sources: Anthropic potential $900B+ valuation round could happen within 2 weeks
item.description: Anthropic is reportedly seeking a valuation of over $900 billion in a new funding round, with investor allocations due soon. This could significantly impact the AI investment landscape.

item.title: Good Luck Getting a Mac Mini for the Next ‘Several Months’
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced that AI adoption has exceeded expectations, leading to supply constraints for the Mac Mini. This reflects the growing demand for AI-capable devices.

item.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
item.description: Apple is facing supply constraints for its Mac Mini, Studio, and Neo due to unexpected AI-driven demand. This may affect availability in the upcoming quarter.

item.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
item.description: Gartner warns that without proper governance, the number of AI agents in enterprises could skyrocket to over 150,000 by 2028, leading to potential chaos.

item.title: Firefox maker torches Google for building Prompt API into browser
item.description: Mozilla criticized Google for integrating an AI API into Chrome, arguing it threatens web openness. This response comes as the technology is already being tested.

item.title: Bot her emails: most modern phishing campaigns are AI-enabled
item.description: A report from KnowBe4 reveals that 86% of phishing campaigns now utilize AI, indicating a significant evolution in cyber threats. This trend could reshape security measures.

item.title: The most severe Linux threat to surface in years catches the world flat-footed
item.description: The CopyFail vulnerability poses a severe threat to Linux systems, affecting multi-tenant servers and CI/CD workflows. This has raised alarms in the tech community.

item.title: Amazon confirmed Prime Day 2026 is coming earlier. Here's everything to know now
item.description: Amazon has announced that Prime Day 2026 will take place in June instead of July, providing early details about the upcoming shopping event.

item.title: Legal AI startup Legora hits $5.6B valuation and its battle with Harvey just got hotter
item.description: Legal AI startup Legora has reached a valuation of $5.6 billion, intensifying competition with rival Harvey as both companies expand rapidly.

item.title: Reinforcement fine-tuning with LLM-as-a-judge
item.description: This article explores how Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback (RLAIF) utilizes large language models (LLMs) as judges in training, particularly with Amazon Nova models. This method enhances the effectiveness of AI training processes.

item.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
item.description: OpenClaw, an open-source project, gained significant traction by early 2026, surpassing 100,000 stars on GitHub, indicating a surge in developer interest and potential impact on organizational AI strategies.

item.title: FBI cyber boss: China's hacker-for-hire ecosystem 'out of control'
item.description: The FBI's cyber division reports that China's hacker-for-hire ecosystem is spiraling out of control, following the extradition of a cyber contractor to the US. This raises concerns about global cybersecurity threats.

item.title: After dissing Anthropic for limiting Mythos, OpenAI restricts access to Cyber, too
item.description: OpenAI is limiting access to its new cybersecurity tool, GPT-5.5 Cyber, initially rolling it out only to critical cyber defenders, reflecting a cautious approach to AI deployment in sensitive areas.

item.title: The craziest part of Musk v. Altman happened while the jury was out of the room
item.description: During the Musk v. Altman trial, unexpected developments occurred, including potential missteps by Musk's legal team, which could influence the case's outcome and its implications for the AI sector.

item.title: Musk v. Altman Kicks Off, DOJ Guts Voting Rights Unit, and Is the AI Job Apocalypse Overhyped?
item.description: The Uncanny Valley podcast discusses the implications of the Musk v. Altman trial, emphasizing its potential impact on OpenAI and the broader AI industry, beyond personal rivalries.

item.title: OpenAI announces new advanced security for ChatGPT accounts, including a partnership with Yubico
item.description: OpenAI is enhancing security for ChatGPT accounts through new opt-in protections and a partnership with Yubico, aimed at preventing unauthorized access and safeguarding user data.

item.title: Elon Musk confirms xAI used OpenAI’s models to train Grok
item.description: Elon Musk testified that his AI startup, xAI, utilized OpenAI's models for training Grok, raising questions about model distillation practices in the industry.

item.title: Introducing Advanced Account Security
item.description: OpenAI introduced Advanced Account Security features, including phishing-resistant login and enhanced protections, to improve user safety and prevent account takeovers.

item.title: International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP) 2026
item.description: Apple will showcase new research at the ICASSP 2026 conference in Barcelona, emphasizing its commitment to advancing signal processing technologies and fostering collaboration in the research community.

item.title: openai_agents_agentmesh 3.2.2
item.description: OpenAI has released version 3.2.2 of AgentMesh, introducing a trust layer for its Agents SDK that enhances function calling and verification processes. This update aims to improve the security and reliability of AI agent interactions.

item.title: openai_agents_agentmesh added to PyPI
item.description: AgentMesh has been added to the Python Package Index (PyPI), making it easier for developers to access the trust layer for OpenAI Agents SDK. This addition supports the integration of trust-gated function calling in AI applications.

item.title: toshl-mcp 1.0.0
item.description: The release of Toshl MCP version 1.0.0 introduces a server that connects the Toshl Finance API to Claude Desktop, enhancing financial data accessibility. This integration could streamline financial management for users of Claude.

item.title: flowsec added to PyPI
item.description: Flowsec has been added to PyPI as a CI/CD pipeline security analyzer, capable of scanning configurations in GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure DevOps for vulnerabilities. This tool aims to bolster security in software development workflows.

item.title: graphattention added to PyPI
item.description: Graphattention has been introduced on PyPI as an efficient implementation of Graph Attention Transformer Networks using PyTorch. This tool is designed to enhance graph-based learning tasks in AI.

item.title: graphattention 0.0.1
item.description: The release of Graphattention version 0.0.1 offers a PyTorch implementation of Graph Attention Transformer Networks, aimed at improving efficiency in graph-based AI tasks. This version is targeted at developers looking for performance enhancements.

item.title: adk-agentmesh added to PyPI
item.description: The public preview of the ADK-AgentMesh integration introduces a governance toolkit for Google ADK, focusing on policy enforcement, trust verification, and audit trails for agents. This aims to enhance the management of AI agents in enterprise environments.

item.title: agentmesh_mcp_trust 3.2.2
item.description: AgentMesh MCP Trust tools have been released, providing a server that exposes management tools for Claude, GPT, and other AI agents. This aims to improve trust management across various AI platforms.

item.title: agentmesh_mcp_trust added to PyPI
item.description: The AgentMesh MCP Trust tools have been added to PyPI, allowing developers to access trust management functionalities for Claude and GPT agents. This addition supports the integration of trust verification in AI systems.

item.title: agentmesh-mcp-server 3.2.2
item.description: The AgentMesh MCP Server version 3.2.2 has been released for Claude Desktop, featuring kernel primitives for code safety verification and trust management. This aims to enhance the operational safety of AI agents.

item.title: Gemini Can Now Generate Files Directly in the Chat
item.description: Gemini now allows users to generate and download specific file formats directly within the chat interface, enhancing user convenience and functionality.

item.title: IBM Launches AI and Quantum Hubs in Illinois and Massachusetts
item.description: IBM has launched two new AI and quantum computing hubs in Illinois and Massachusetts, aiming to create 750 jobs in various tech fields.

item.title: scartissue-mcp 0.1.0
item.description: The scartissue-mcp 0.1.0 is a new PR review agent designed to alert developers when pull requests reintroduce historical bug patterns.

item.title: scartissue-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: The scartissue-mcp has been added to PyPI, providing a tool for developers to manage pull requests and maintain code quality.

item.title: Meta shares slide as tech giant hikes AI spending forecast, warns of youth social media backlash
item.description: Meta has raised its AI spending forecast, warning that legal challenges in the EU and US could significantly affect its business.

item.title: Meta just bumped its 2026 capex forecast up to as much as $145 billion for the AI boom—and investors flinched
item.description: Meta's CEO announced a significant increase in 2026 capital expenditure for AI, leading to a 6% drop in the company's stock after hours.

item.title: Rogo raises $160M to speed up financial analysis with AI agents
item.description: Rogo Technologies has raised $160 million in Series D funding to enhance financial analysis through AI, led by Kleiner Perkins.

item.title: Smashing Security podcast #465: This developer wanted to cheat at Roblox. It cost millions
item.description: A developer's attempt to cheat at Roblox led to a $2 million data breach, affecting numerous organizations due to a compromised work laptop.

item.title: What All 12 Zodiac Signs Need To Know For May's Full Flower Moon
item.description: An article discussing astrological insights for the upcoming Full Flower Moon in Scorpio, providing a lighthearted perspective.

item.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it | TechCrunch
item.description: Microsoft reported over 20 million paid users of its Copilot, indicating strong engagement despite previous skepticism about its usage.

item.title: vertex-deepseek 1.1.2
item.description: Vertex released version 1.1.2 of DeepSeek, a proxy for Claude Code CLI, enhancing its capabilities. This update is part of ongoing improvements in AI tools.

item.title: vertex-deepseek 1.1.1
item.description: Vertex launched version 1.1.1 of DeepSeek, a proxy for Claude Code CLI, contributing to the evolution of AI development tools. This version continues to refine its features.

item.title: Apple researchers built an AI that tests several ideas in parallel before answering
item.description: Apple researchers introduced a new AI framework that tests multiple ideas simultaneously to enhance responses in math reasoning and code generation. This innovation could significantly improve LLM performance.

item.title: vertex-deepseek 1.1.0
item.description: Vertex released version 1.1.0 of DeepSeek, a proxy for Claude Code CLI, as part of its ongoing updates. This version aims to improve user experience and functionality.

item.title: Sundar Pichai teases I/O 2026 as Google starts selling TPUs and YouTube Premium grows
item.description: During Alphabet's Q1 2026 earnings call, CEO Sundar Pichai hinted at upcoming announcements for the I/O 2026 event, alongside news of growing YouTube Premium subscriptions and TPU sales.

item.title: Elon Musk tells court he was a "fool" for funding OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk admitted in court that he regretted funding OpenAI, claiming the organization strayed from its original nonprofit mission aimed at human progress.

item.title: vertex-deepseek 1.0.1
item.description: Vertex released version 1.0.1 of DeepSeek, a proxy for Claude Code CLI, marking another step in its development. This version focuses on enhancing existing features.

item.title: Google says it's open to putting ads in Gemini
item.description: Google's chief business officer stated that the company is open to integrating advertisements into its Gemini AI model, suggesting a potential revenue stream.

item.title: langchain-acp 0.9.1
item.description: LangChain released version 0.9.1, allowing users to expose LangChain and LangGraph agents through the ACP Kit, enhancing integration capabilities.

item.title: vertex-deepseek 1.0.0
item.description: Vertex launched version 1.0.0 of DeepSeek, a proxy for Claude Code CLI, marking the initial release in this series. This version sets the foundation for future updates.
